records.ety: [OE. slan, sl<?/n, sleen, slee, AS. slen to strike, beat, slay; akin to OFries. slā, D. slaan, OS. & OHG. slahan, G. schlagen, Icel. slā, Dan. slaae, Sw. sl<?/, Goth. slahan; perhaps akin to L. lacerare to tear to pieces, Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/, E. lacerate. Cf. Slaughter, Sledge a hammer, Sley.]
records.morph: [imp. Slew (?); p. p. Slain (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Slaying.]

defs: To put to death with a weapon, or by violence; hence, to kill; to put an end to; to destroy.
quotes: With this sword then will I slay you both.
I will slay the last of them with the sword.
I'll slay more gazers than the basilisk.
